steps.title: Find the constant b using the initial condition
steps.content:
At $t = 0$, the object is dropped. Substituting into the function:

$$h(0) = -16(0)^2 + b = b$$

Since the object is 40.96 feet above the ground when dropped:

$$b = 40.96$$

The complete function is $h(t) = -16t^2 + 40.96$.

steps.title: Solve for time when the object hits the ground
steps.content:
The object hits the ground when $h(t) = 0$:

$$-16t^2 + 40.96 = 0$$

Solving for $t^2$:

$$t^2 = \frac{40.96}{16} = 2.56 = \frac{64}{25}$$

Taking the positive square root:

$$t = \sqrt{\frac{64}{25}} = \frac{8}{5} = 1.6 \text{ seconds}$$
